panels.description: Medium shot: Young Voltaire in a dusty, dimly lit study, surrounded by stacks of books and scientific instruments, staring at the window with a look of existential longing.

bubbles.text: Despite the discoveries of modern philosophers, I remain unsatisfied. Like Newton, I feel I am but a child picking shells beside a vast ocean of truth.

panels.description: Split composition: On one side, a peasant observing a field; on the other, a weary scholar dissecting a strange specimen. High contrast, academic atmosphere.

bubbles.text: The learned philosopher knows little more than the peasant. Nature's true lineaments remain a mystery.

panels.description: Close up: Young Voltaire intently reading ancient leather-bound alchemy texts by candlelight, eyes reflecting the firelight, expressions of fanatic dedication.

bubbles.text: These books hold the keys. I shall become their disciple and master the elixir of life.

panels.description: Low angle shot: Voltaire waving a charcoal wand over a circle of strange symbols, looking desperate and frustrated as the smoke clears to show nothing happens.

bubbles.text: My failure... surely it is my own inexperience, not the fault of the great masters.

panels.description: Dramatic wide shot: A violent purple-white bolt of lightning striking an ancient oak tree near a manor house in the rain. The tree is splintering into a blasted stump.

bubbles.text: A sudden stream of fire. In a flash, the beautiful oak was gone, replaced by a blasted stump.
